info.title: AS iRebal API
info.description:
Allows iRebal users to update their Models and Households (portfolios) from a third-party software provider's user interface.

The security model contains a list of securities with target allocations. In contrast, a blended model (aka Model of Models) is a blend of multiple security models with target allocations that automatically reflect changes made to the underlying security models.

Some common use cases that can be completed via this API are:
  + Retrieve iRebal user models, analyze and edit them, and push those
changes back to iRebal.  + Create Households by associating multiple unassigned accounts to one new
portfolio, then assign the model by Model ID.  + Retrieve iRebal user's portfolios to understand which accounts are
householded and what their assigned model is in iRebal.

**Available actions** — the ability to create, edit, and retrieve the following:
  + Portfolios
  + Models
  + Blended models

securityDescription.example: EATON VANCE LRG CAP VAL FD CL I
EATON VANCE LRG CAP VAL FD CL I

globalBands.description: Indicates whether to use global rebalancing bands. Requires `rebalanceBandMinPercent` and `rebalanceBandMaxPercent` be passed if false.

rebalanceBandMinPercent.description: Must be passed if `globalBands` is false and must be less than or equal to `targetPercent`.

rebalanceBandMaxPercent.description: Must be passed if `globalBands` is false and must be greater than or equal to `targetPercent`.

securityDescription.example: EATON VANCE LRG CAP VAL FD CL I

rebalanceBandMaxPercent.description: Must be passed if `globalBands` is false and must be greater than or equal to `targetPercent`.

status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this occurrence of the error.

title.description: The short title describing the category of the error.

detail.description: The error description associated with this occurrence of the error. Provides details of the error. The actual error may vary.

ErrorInfoSource.description: The optional extended error info pointing to the source that triggered the error.

pointer.description: The JSON pointer (RFC6901) to the associated entity in the request document (e.g., "/data" for a primary data object or "/data/attributes/{property name}" for a specific attribute).

parameter.description: The string indicating which URL query parameter caused the error.

header.description: The string indicating which request header caused the error.
